findings.title: Filtering `WebFetch`/`WebSearch`/`TodoWrite`/`MultiEdit` hides all useful Discord-visible context
findings.description: The new suppression list is safe for `Read`/`Grep`/`Glob` because `format_tool_use()` already shows the file path or pattern, but that is not true for the newly added tools. In this formatter, `WebFetch`, `WebSearch`, `TodoWrite`, and `MultiEdit` fall through to the generic `-# 🔧 **ToolName**` rendering, so once their successful results are skipped the user no longer sees the fetched URL, search query, todo change, or edited file. That is a user-visible regression in both the fast-complete and streaming paths, because both now rely on the same filtering rule.
findings.suggestion: Either keep successful results for those tools visible until they have dedicated `format_tool_use()` summaries, or add explicit formatter arms for `WebFetch`, `WebSearch`, `TodoWrite`, and `MultiEdit` before suppressing their success output.

findings.title: `MultiEdit` is filtered like an edit tool but not formatted like one on failure
findings.description: After this change, successful `MultiEdit` results are hidden, so failures become the only result users will see. However `format_tool_result_with_name()` only treats `Edit` and `Write` as edit-like and therefore does not use diff formatting for `MultiEdit` errors. That makes `MultiEdit` inconsistent with the other file-editing tools and harder to inspect in Discord when something goes wrong.
findings.suggestion: Include `MultiEdit` anywhere edit tools get special handling, especially the `is_edit` check used to select the diff code fence, and consider adding a dedicated `format_tool_use()` arm so the target file is shown as well.

findings.title: The suppression list is duplicated across fast and streaming paths with no shared guardrail
findings.description: The same tool-name allowlist now has to stay in sync in `format_response()` and `send_event_to_discord()`. This diff updated both manually, but only the formatter path has test coverage in the patch, so a future edit can easily make fast-complete and streaming Discord output diverge again. That is exactly the kind of bot-specific inconsistency that is painful to notice only in production.
findings.suggestion: Extract the predicate into a shared helper such as `should_hide_successful_tool_result(tool_name)` and add coverage for both the aggregated (`format_response`) and streaming (`send_event_to_discord`) behaviors.

summary: The diff updates both filtering sites consistently, but it suppresses several tools whose formatter output is still too generic, and the duplicated filter logic remains easy to drift.
